pages.raw_text:
At mass meeting of about 500
people. held in the municipal court
room. under the auspices of the
socialist party. a resolution with
drawing patronage from the apple
show was passed. The resolution
follows:


Whereas, There is a struggle
going on in the city of Spokane be-
tween the labor organizations of
said city and it's municipal govern
ment for the right of free speech
and free assemblage on the public
streets. and,


Whereas, The Western Fedora
ton of Miners of the Occur d'Alene
district have shown their sympathy
and interest in the fight of their
working class comrades in Spokane
by placing boycott on all goods
and manufactures produced and
handled by Spokane business men
who are opposed to the labor I'M
ions in this city inv the struggle
from those who support them, thus
making it necessary to strike at
the commercial organizations as a
while, therefore, be it


Resolved, BY the citizens of Spo-
kane who believe in the right of
free speech and free assemblage
guaranteed by the constitution of
the United States, that we do
hereby singly and collectively with
draw all support and patronage
from the National Apple Show, now
about to open in this city, and all
local wholesale and retail mer-
chants where the goods purchased
can be possibly secured from the
farmers adjacent to Spokane or
from the east, and, be is further


Resolved, That we request the
endorsement of this action by all
the members of organized labor in
this city and surrounding country,
as well as the organized farmers of
the Inland Empire, this showing
the workers of the Occur d'Alenes
that not all the citizens of Spokane
